mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This command sets up the repository for installing Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command applies the necessary CRDs from the specified release.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which manages the deployment of the operator.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> This command deploys the Cluster API components into the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Look for the Cluster API components to be in the Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API Helm release.
> This command deletes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster resources. After upgrading, verify that the components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup existing cluster resources.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster CRD may not be installed or is not available in the specified version.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Cluster API CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may not be reachable, or the kubeconfig may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure the API server is running: kubectl config view; kubectl cluster-info

troubleshooting.title: Pod 'cluster-api-controller-manager' is CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be a misconfiguration in the Cluster API components or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod for errors: kubectl logs -n kube-system <pod-name>; ensure all dependencies are installed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster API components may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Cluster API components and verify the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for custom infrastructure providers, added new metrics for monitoring cluster health.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for legacy infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your custom resource definitions to match the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API components running in the kube-system namespace with all pods in the Running state when everything is working.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
